channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: This German church is the tallest in the world. Until Spain's La Sagrada Familia is done, anyway
item.description: ULM, Germany (AP) — The Ulmer Münster in southern Germany is the world's tallest church. For now, anyway. The Gothic-style Lutheran church's reign — begun in May 31, 1890 — could end in 2025, when La Sagrada Familia Basilica's “Tower of Jesus Christ” in Spain is set to be completed.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How Black leaders in New York are grappling with Eric Adams and representation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It wasn’t a shock to many Black New Yorkers that Mayor Eric Adams has surrounded himself with African American civil rights leaders, clergy and grassroots activists since his indictment last week on federal bribery charges.
item.dc:creator: AARON MORRISON and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The flood of ghost guns is slowing after regulation. It's also being challenged in the Supreme Court
item.description: The Supreme Court is hearing arguments in a case over a federal regulation aimed at reducing weapons often referred to as ghost guns.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Human connections bring hope in North Carolina after devastation of Helene
item.description: BLACK MOUNTAIN, N.C. (AP) — Sarah Vekasi is a potter who runs a store in Black Mountain, North Carolina, called Sarah Sunshine Pottery, named after her normally bubbly personality. But these days she’s struggling with the trauma of Hurricane Helene and uncertainty about the future of her business.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, ERIK VERDUZCO and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ranked choice voting is on the ballot in several states
item.description: Ranked choice voting could be coming to states like Colorado, Nevada and Oregon. Here's what to know.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump in new memoir calls abortion a 'woman's fundamental right': Report
item.description: “Why should anyone other than the woman herself have the power to determine what she does with her own body?" asks the former first lady.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Doctor who helped supply Matthew Perry ketamine pleads guilty
item.description: A San Diego doctor charged in connection with Matthew Perry’s fatal overdose pleaded guilty Wednesday to conspiring to distribute the surgical anesthetic ketamine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bank of America is having service issues and account access problems: Downdetector
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Some Bank of America users reported problems accessing their funds online Wednesday. Some online services appear to be down, according to Downdetector. A message on the Bank of America app states, “Accounts Temporarily Unavailable.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: After employees swept away in Helene flooding, Tennessee factory under investigation
item.description: Several employees have accused the company of not letting them leave soon enough as flooding set in on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Keeling, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tribes celebrate the end of the largest dam removal project in US history
item.description: The largest dam removal project in U.S. history was completed Wednesday, marking a major victory for tribes in the region who fought for decades to free hundreds of miles of the Klamath River near the California-Oregon border.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Condoms aren’t a fact of life for young Americans — they’re an afterthought
item.description: Fewer young people are having sex, but the teens and young adults who are sexually active aren't using condoms as regularly, if at all. And people ages 15 to 24 made up half of new chlamydia, gonorrhea and syphilis cases in 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pilot flying Helene rescue missions in NC ordered out, threatened with arrest
item.description: The fire official told him to tell any other pilots he knew they would also be arrested if they came back.
item.dc:creator: Jody Barr, KRON4

item.title: Hurricanes like Helene are deadly when they strike and keep killing for years to come
item.description: Hurricanes in the United States end up hundreds of times deadlier than the government calculates, contributing to more American deaths than car accidents or all the nation's wars, a new study said. The average storm hitting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pennsylvania Republican in key swing-state Senate race backs using military to fight fentanyl
item.description: STEELTON, Pa. (AP) — The Republican challenger trying to flip the U.S. Senate seat in swing-state Pennsylvania said he’ll press for U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Helene's flooding swept away 11 workers at a Tennessee factory. Now the state is investigating
item.description: Tennessee state authorities are investigating the company that owns a plastics factory where 11 workers were swept away by cataclysmic flooding unleashed by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Cedar Attanasio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance on carbon emissions and climate change: 'Let's just say that's true'
item.description: “One of the things that I've noticed some of our Democratic friends talking a lot about is a concern about carbon emissions — this idea that carbon emissions drive all the climate change,” Vance said.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Kirk becomes a hurricane as North Carolina reels from Helene
item.description: Desperate residents of the storm-battered mountains of western North Carolina lined up for water and food, hunted for cellphone signals and slogged buckets from creeks to flush toilets days after Hurricane Helene’s remnants deluged the region.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: "So first of all, injured. What does injured mean? Injured means — you mean because they had a headache? Because the bombs never hit the fort," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: "So first of all, injured. What does injured mean? Injured means — you mean because they had a headache? Because the bombs never hit the fort," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WWII destroyer found off California coast 76 years later
item.description: A surviving ship from WWII that was used by both Allied and Axis forces in the Pacific and that was given a ceremonial burial at sea has finally been located after more than seven decades underwater.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Nobert, Fox 5

item.title: School board to pay $575K to teacher fired for not using transgender student's pronouns
item.description: A Virginia school board has agreed to pay a former high school teacher more than $500,000 in damages and attorneys' fees as part of a legal settlement after the instructor's dismissal in 2018 for refusing to use a transgender student's pronouns.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: California firefighters in Hurricane Helene relief team recovering after crash in Texas
item.description: In a statement on Facebook, the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department thanked the community for their support during this difficult time.
item.dc:creator: Christa Swanson, KRON4

item.title: The US could see shortages and higher retail prices if a dockworkers strike drags on
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. ports from Maine to Texas shut down Tuesday when the union representing about 45,000 dockworkers went on strike for the first time since 1977. Workers began walking picket lines early Tuesday, picketing near ports all along the East Coast.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Montana man gets 6 months in prison for cloning giant sheep and breeding it
item.description: GREAT FALLS, Mont. (AP) — An 81-year-old Montana man was sentenced Monday to six months in federal prison for illegally using tissue and testicles from large sheep hunted in Central Asia and the U.S. to create hybrid sheep for captive trophy hunting in Texas and Minnesota. U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMY BETH HANSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs accused of sexual misconduct by 120 people, attorney says
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — An attorney said Tuesday he is representing 120 accusers who have come forward with sexual misconduct allegations against Sean “Diddy” Combs, the hip-hop mogul who is awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Is the food in the fridge still good? California wants to end the guessing game
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California wants to help end the everyday household debate over whether the food in the fridge is still good to eat. Food labels that say “sell by” or “best before” are misleading because they have no universal meaning under current laws.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A chemical cloud moving around Atlanta's suburbs prompts a new shelter-in-place alert
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Residents east of Atlanta were again warned Tuesday to take shelter where they are if shifting winds push the still-billowing chemical cloud from a chlorine factory fire over their neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: My car was submerged in floodwaters. Should I start it?
item.description: Storm surges and flooding from Hurricane Helene have caused widespread devastation across parts of the Southeastern U.S., in some cases leaving homes and cars completely underwater.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Israel attacked by Iran: American Jewish leaders say Islamist regime will pay for missile strike
item.description: Jewish American leaders on Tuesday afternoon reacted to Iran's unprecedented missile attacks against Israel and threats in that region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Experienced BASE jumper dies near Utah peak, sheriff says
item.description: Jonathan Bizilia, a 27-year-old BASE jumper, was found dead near Willard Peak in Utah last week after failing to check in with a friend, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A whopping 40 trillion gallons of rain fell on the South thanks to Helene and other storms
item.description: That's enough to fill the Dallas Cowboys' stadium 51,000 times, or 60 million Olympic-size swimming pools.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Murders, mayhem and officer's gunfire lead to charges at Brooklyn jail where 'Diddy' is held
item.description: Criminal charges unsealed Monday offer a fresh window into violence and dysfunction that has plagued the Brooklyn federal jail where Sean “Diddy” Combs and Sam Bankman-Fried are locked up.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, KRON4

item.title: Gavin Creel, Tony Award-winning veteran of Broadway musicals, dies at 48
item.description: Creel's publicist said he died at his home in Manhattan of metastatic melanotic peripheral nerve sheath sarcoma.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Fox 5

item.title: A dockworkers strike could shut down Port Tampa Bay. Will it affect holiday shopping?
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. ports from Maine to Texas could shut down if a union representing about 45,000 dockworkers carries through with a threatened strike early Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Maya Rudolph as Harris and Dana Carvey as Biden open the 50th season of 'Saturday Night Live'
item.description: “Saturday Night Live” began its 50th season with a parade of former co-stars, including Maya Rudolph as Vice President Kamala Harris, Andy Samberg as her husband Doug Emhoff and Dana Carvey as President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stuck NASA astronauts welcome SpaceX capsule that'll bring them home next year
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — The two astronauts stuck at the International Space Station since June welcomed their new ride home with Sunday’s arrival of a SpaceX capsule.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, ABC 4
